rows.source_quote: Q4 FY24 guidance for FY25-Q1: revenue $850-900M.

Cyclical & customer concentration: Top-10 customers ~50% of revenue; royalty rate fluctuates with smartphone shipment cycles.
Smartphone end-market saturation could pressure royalty per-unit growth in core ARMv9 stack.
Geopolitical & supply chain: China revenue exposure (~25%) under risk if export controls escalate.
TSMC fab concentration is upstream — ARM IP not directly exposed but customer royalty timing can shift.
Valuation: Premium ~105x P/E FY1 vs semiconductor peer median ~49x — multiple expansion thesis is exhausted.

metadata.method: Quarter-over-quarter guidance vs actual revenue from ARM 6-K press releases.

fundamentals: Revenue +19.9% YoY (strong for Information Technology; sector strong-bar 15%)
Net margin 32.4% (best-in-class for Information Technology; sector strong-bar 18%)
valuation: PE 105.1× vs peer median 35.8× (193% premium to peers)
✓ Quality-premium pattern: high PE + above 200-DMA + >4% growth + +10% 1Y
→ V floored at 55 (premium deserved by defensive cash flow)

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $1.49B in FY26 Q4, +19.9% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Margin context: 32.4% net margin (35.1% op margin) — expansion likely.
Next earnings: 2026-08-07 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is constructive — consensus is achievable based on current run-rate.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $5B (+22.3% TTM YoY) — strong secular tailwind.
8-quarter run-rate CAGR: +30.1% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if growth durability + margin expansion both hold.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 90.2% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 1.11 — balanced flow.
30-day implied move: ±19.8% ($170.53 – $254.77) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -67.25pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $100.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $202 (5% OTM). High IV (90%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 70.0% across 10 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 10 dates spanning 101–619 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±38.1% ($131.60 – $293.70).
220-day implied move: ±54.3% ($97.24 – $328.06) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $196 (-7.8% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-20.3pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $191 put / short $234 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
